name: Maresme Coast Axis
name_original: Paseo Marítimo – vuelta por Sant Pol de Mar y Pineda de Mar
description: This easy 18.8-mile road cycling route takes you along the beautiful Mediterranean coast through Sant Pol de Mar and Pineda de Mar.
region_intro: Road cycling around Sant Pol De Mar offers a diverse landscape, from coastal stretches to challenging mountain ascents. The region is characterized by green hills, dense forests, and the Montnegre i el Corredor Natural Reserve, providing varied terrain for cyclists. Routes often feature panoramic views of the Maresme coast and extend inland through Mediterranean woodland. This area provides a mix of relatively flat coastal paths and more demanding climbs into the hills. Best road cycling routes around Sant Pol De Mar The most popular road cycling route is Cycling the Maresme Mountains, a difficult 56.8 miles (91.3 km) trail that takes 4 hours 7 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain through the region's hilly interior. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is From Carbonella surrounding Arenys de Mar - circular near the sea, an easy 15.0 miles (24.2 km) path. This route offers a coastal experience with moderate elevation changes. Local road cyclists also love the Paseo Marítimo – return trip through Sant Pol de Mar and Pineda de Mar, an 18.8 miles (30.2 km) trail leading through coastal areas, often completed in about 1 hour 20 minutes. Road cycling around Sant Pol De Mar is defined by green hills, coastal views, and forested roads. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy coastal loops to challenging mountain climbs. The routes in Sant Pol De Mar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 80 reviews. More than 8,900 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Sant Pol De Mar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Sant Pol De Mar?
A: There are over 45 road cycling routes available around Sant Pol De Mar, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Sant Pol De Mar?
A: The region around Sant Pol De Mar is suitable for road cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and less crowded routes. While winter cycling is possible, be mindful of cooler temperatures, especially in the mountains. Summers can be hot, making early morning or late afternoon rides preferable.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes for beginners in Sant Pol De Mar?
A: Yes, Sant Pol De Mar offers several easy road cycling routes ideal for beginners or those seeking a relaxed ride. Options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000912410/from-carbonella-surrounding-arenys-de-mar-circular-near-the-sea" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Carbonella surrounding Arenys de Mar - circular near the sea&lt;/a&gt; (24.2 km) and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995565449/paseo-maritimo-return-trip-through-sant-pol-de-mar-and-pineda-de-mar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paseo Marítimo – return trip through Sant Pol de Mar and Pineda de Mar&lt;/a&gt; (30.2 km) are relatively flat and offer coastal experiences.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders in Sant Pol De Mar?
A: Absolutely. For experienced cyclists, Sant Pol De Mar provides challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1350960254/cycling-the-maresme-mountains" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cycling the Maresme Mountains&lt;/a&gt; route, for example, covers 91.3 km with over 900 meters of ascent. Other demanding options include tackling ascents like Collsacreu or exploring routes that pass through the Montnegre Natural Park.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in the Sant Pol De Mar area are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000912410/from-carbonella-surrounding-arenys-de-mar-circular-near-the-sea" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Carbonella surrounding Arenys de Mar - circular near the sea&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39551142" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Pol de Mar – Canet de Mar Beach loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features and landscapes can I expect to see while road cycling?
A: Road cycling around Sant Pol De Mar offers a diverse landscape. You'll encounter green hills, lush forests, and the extensive Montnegre i el Corredor Natural Reserve. Many routes provide stunning panoramic views of the Maresme coast, while others wind through Mediterranean woodland and past pristine sandy beaches.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or viewpoints along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, the routes offer various points of interest. You can cycle past significant mountain passes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4552641" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Can Benet Pass&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/887153" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collsacreu Pass from Vallgorguina&lt;/a&gt;. Natural monuments like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/108840" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montalt&lt;/a&gt; and unique rock formations such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5292322" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Horseshoe Stone&lt;/a&gt; can also be found nearby, offering scenic stops.

Q: Is there parking available for cyclists in Sant Pol De Mar?
A: Sant Pol De Mar, being a popular outdoor destination, generally offers parking options. You can find public parking areas within the town, and many routes start from accessible points where parking is available. It's advisable to check specific starting points for the most convenient parking.

Q: Can I use public transport with my bike in Sant Pol De Mar?
A: Public transport options in the region, such as local trains and buses, often have restrictions or specific times for carrying bicycles. It's recommended to check with the local transport providers directly for their current policies regarding bikes, especially during peak hours or seasons.

Q: Are there cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the routes?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in Sant Pol De Mar pass through or near towns and villages where you can find cafes, restaurants, and shops to refuel. Coastal routes, in particular, offer numerous opportunities for refreshment stops. Inland routes might require a bit more planning for breaks.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling routes in Sant Pol De Mar?
A: While many routes are geared towards road cycling, some of the easier, flatter coastal paths can be suitable for families with older children or those comfortable with road riding. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995565449/paseo-maritimo-return-trip-through-sant-pol-de-mar-and-pineda-de-mar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paseo Marítimo&lt;/a&gt; route is a good example of a relatively flat option.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Sant Pol De Mar?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 80 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, from tranquil coastlines to challenging mountain passes, and the stunning natural scenery that makes every ride unique.
tips: Small coastal town with beautiful beaches, not as crowded and hectic as the neighboring town of Calella.

Punta del Tordera beach is located in front of the Malgrat de Mar camping area and reaches the mouth of the Tordera river.

It has services such as toilets for people with disabilities, an aid station, showers and an amphibious chair for people with reduced mobility to bathe.

faqs.question: What is the typical duration for cycling this route?
faqs.answer: This easy road cycling route is approximately 30.2 km (18.8 miles) long and can typically be completed in about 1 hour and 20 minutes. Its relatively flat profile makes for a relaxed pace.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, the "Paseo Marítimo – return trip through Sant Pol de Mar and Pineda de Mar" is classified as an easy road ride. It features mostly well-paved surfaces and a relatively flat path, making it accessible for cyclists of all fitness levels, including beginners.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The terrain primarily consists of asphalt and paved sections, incorporating state roads, general roads, streets, and dedicated cycleways. It's a smooth ride with light traffic, offering a comfortable experience along the coast.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll enjoy diverse scenery, including coastal views, natural areas, and urban environments. The route passes through the charming towns of Sant Pol de Mar, known for its beautiful beaches and promenade, and Pineda de Mar. You can also see the Church of Sant Jaume, Punta del Tordera Beach, and the Pineda de Mar Beachfront.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Maresme coast generally offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn provide mild temperatures, ideal for a comfortable ride. Summer can be warm, but the coastal breeze often makes it enjoyable, especially in the mornings or late afternoons.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: Both Sant Pol de Mar and Pineda de Mar offer various parking options, including areas near their train stations and along the beachfronts. Look for public parking facilities within these towns to start your ride conveniently.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route itself is a public road and cycleway, specific regulations for dogs on beaches and in public spaces vary by municipality. It's best to check local rules for Sant Pol de Mar and Pineda de Mar regarding dogs, especially if you plan to stop at beaches or enter establishments with your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or entrance fees to cycle the "Paseo Marítimo – return trip through Sant Pol de Mar and Pineda de Mar" route. It uses public roads and cycleways.

faqs.question: Are there cafes or restaurants along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, both Sant Pol de Mar and Pineda de Mar have promenades dotted with eateries, cafes, and restaurants. You'll find plenty of options to stop for refreshments, a meal, or a coffee break as you cycle through these coastal towns.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other official cycling paths. Notably, it covers significant sections of the Eix de la Costa, Eix Central del Maresme, and Tourdera. You'll also find smaller portions overlapping with Ruta de les Torretes and TransCatalunya.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Given its easy difficulty, mostly flat profile, and well-paved surfaces, this route is very suitable for families. The dedicated cycleways and light traffic sections make it a safe and enjoyable option for cycling with children.
